render_data.title: Conductivity due to silver ions
render_data.prompt: Add NaCl from the burette: conductivity stays high (many Ag⁺ ions) then drops sharply between 20–25 cm³ as Ag⁺ is removed as AgCl precipitate, reaching a low plateau.
render_data.answer: Sharp drop in conductivity between 20–25 cm³ as Ag⁺ ions react with Cl⁻ to form AgCl precipitate.

image_prompt: SVG: two-panel pH titration curves (First titration and Second titration). Each panel y-axis pH with ticks 0,7,14; x-axis 0–30 with shared caption 'Volume of chemical X / cm³'. Curve starts high (~13) and plunges sharply through the equivalence point; dashed vertical line marks the equivalence volume — 22.75 cm³ (First) and 27.25 cm³ (Second).

SVG: scatter graph, Mass of CO₂ produced / g (y, 0.00–2.00 ticks every 0.20) vs Volume of chemical X / cm³ (x, 0–45 ticks every 5). Points from (18,1.80) declining roughly linearly to (40,1.31) with a best-fit straight line. Teal × markers.

SVG: four candidate option graphs of the same Volume CO₂ vs Time data (10–150 s; volumes 13.0–21.3 cm³, anomaly low value 20.1 at 110 s). Graph 1 line (Volume y 0–25 vs Time x 0–160); Graph 2 bar (Volume y 10–22 vs Time); Graph 3 bar with axes swapped (Time y 0–160 vs Volume x); Graph 4 scatter (Time y vs Volume x). Teal lines/bars and × markers.

SVG: line graph 'Graph 1' — Blood lead levels / mg dm⁻³ (y, 0–18 every 2) vs Year (x, 1974–2000 every 2). Starts ~16 mg dm⁻³ in 1976, sharp fall after 1979 to ~9.5 by 1980, then gradual decline to ~2 by 1999. Teal line with × markers.

SVG: grouped bar chart 'Graph 2' — Percentage of children with learning difficulties / % (y, 0–100 every 10) vs Blood lead level / µg dm⁻³ categories (1–4, 5–9, 10–14, 15–19, 20–44, 45–60, 60+). Two series: 'No learning difficulties' (teal) falling 90→65 %, and 'Learning difficulties' (orange) rising 10→36 % across the categories. Legend included.

SVG: original oil-paint still life of a vase of sunflowers (evocative of Van Gogh, not a copy), warm yellow palette, framed, vase labelled 'Vincent'. Copyright-safe recreation replacing the IB painting photo.

render_data.title: Structural formula of ethanoic acid
render_data.prompt: Build ethanoic acid (CH₃COOH): select the structure with a methyl group bonded to a carboxyl group (–COOH, i.e. C=O and O–H). Name the functional group.
render_data.answer: Ethanoic acid is CH₃–C(=O)–OH; the functional group is the carboxyl (carboxylic acid, –COOH) group.

image_prompt: SVG: cross-section of a solar still in a pit — plastic sheet draped into a V over contaminated water, evaporation arrows rising, condensation dripping to a central container, rock weight at the low point. Labels: Drinking tube, Plastic sheet, Leaves, Dirt or sand holding down the sheet, Rock weight, Container.
SVG: glass bottle of murky river water with layered contents and leader labels — Mud and smaller particles (top), Sticks, Leaves, Stones (bottom).

render_data.title: Select the equipment for filtration
render_data.prompt: Drag the equipment needed to carry out filtration into the set-up; leave the equipment that is not needed in the tray. Correct set-up: filter paper inside a funnel, with a beaker below to collect the filtrate.
render_data.answer: Filtration needs a filter funnel, a filter-paper cone (inside the funnel) and a beaker below to collect the filtrate.

image_prompt: SVG: titration set-up — burette of NaCl in a clamp stand, gloved hand holding a pipette, conductivity meter and probe dipping into a conical flask. Labels: Burette containing NaCl, Pipette, Clamp stand, Conductivity meter and probe, Conical flask.
SVG: conductivity meter reading 2700 mV with probe in a conical flask of untreated water, beside two bottles labelled 'Treated with carbon filter' and 'Treated with silver filter'.

topic: Nepal fuel types and usage table
image_prompt: SVG: row of illustrated fuel icons (Wood, Dung, LPG, Biogas, Crop waste, Kerosene, Charcoal, Electricity) above a data table of percentage fuel use in urban vs rural areas; LPG-urban and Dung-rural cells left blank for the student to fill.
SVG: conical flask with burning fuel inside, bung and delivery tube leading to a gas syringe that collects the CO₂ produced. Labels: Burning fuel in flask, Delivery tube, Gas syringe.

topic: Acid-rain statue and rate data tables
image_prompt: SVG: original illustration of an acid-rain-weathered calcium-carbonate statue (pitted surface), the equation CaCO₃ + 2HCl → CaCl₂ + H₂O + CO₂, and two student data tables of Time vs Volume CO₂ (mixed cm³/dm³ units) reproduced exactly.

SVG: illustrated comparison table of four rechargeable phone batteries (Li-Poly, Li-ion, NiCd, NiMH) with drawn battery icons and rows for technology, weight, memory effect, environmental impact, cost, recharge cycles and charge time.
